title: 10 best herbal creams for inflamed mouth

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal milk thistle creams contain silymarin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain caused by inflamed mouths, such as gingivitis or periodontal disease.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in milk thistle creams can help protect the mouth tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammatory conditions like mouth sores or ulcers.

Wound-healing: Silymarin and other bioactive compounds in milk thistle creams may promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms against infection, helping to soothe and calm inflamed mouth tissues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The silymarin compound found in milk thistle has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth. By applying a cream containing silymarin to the affected area, it can help to soothe and calm inflammation, reducing pain and discomfort. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing mouth sores, gum inflammation, or other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid complex that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, alleviating inflamed tissues and promoting healing.
Lignans: Lignans, particularly silibinin and isosilibinin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help combat oral inflammation, soothe irritated tissues, and prevent further damage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce oral inflammation, prevent infections, and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain a high concentration of silymarin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed mouth tissues.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they possess flavonoids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain bioactive compounds that can help calm inflammation and promote wound healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds and leaves at dawn to ensure maximum potency retention for later use.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Combine 10 grams of dried silybum marianum leaves with 20 ml of carrier oil in a glass jar to create the base mixture.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 2 hours to facilitate the extraction of active compounds.
Strain and filter the resulting infusion through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Pot marigold contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums and mouth ulcers.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The herbal extract has anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit enzymes involved in inflammation, reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouths, such as canker sores and gum inflammation.

Wound-Healing Properties: Pot marigold's flavonoids and other bioactive compounds have been shown to enhance wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue integrity, and accelerating the repair of damaged oral mucosa.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, calendula's antibacterial properties help combat oral infections, promoting a healthy environment for tissue repair and regeneration. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective solution for mouth inflammation, helping to alleviate discomfort and support overall oral well-being.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamed mouth.

Carotenoids: Specifically, lutein and zeaxanthin are carotenoids in Calendula officinalis that possess antioxidant properties, which help to protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat infections that may be contributing to mouth inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal inflamed oral tissues.
Leaves: Utilized for their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the mouth.
Seeds: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat mouth infections and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers and soak them in 1 cup of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the hot mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antimicrobial properties: The creams also possess antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause inflammation in the mouth, such as streptococcus and candida species.

Mucoprotective effects: Licorice extracts contain saponins that protect mucous membranes from damage, reducing the risk of ulcers and inflammation in the mouth and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with inflamed mouth because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The root of the licorice plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various oral health issues, including mouth ulcers and gum inflammation. The active ingredient, glycyrrhizin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and promote healing, providing relief from mouth pain and discomfort caused by inflammation. This makes licorice creams an effective natural remedy for oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and enzymes, reducing inflammation and swelling in the mouth.
Licoricidin: It helps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm inflamed tissues in the mouth.
Isoliquiritigenin: It helps by demonstrating anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, reducing the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to oral inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Utilized for their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Leaves: Employed for their ability to provide relief from mouth inflammation due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Weigh out 20 grams of beeswax and melt it in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Combine the infused oil and melted beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars and allow it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and prevent damage to oral tissues caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflamed mouth conditions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with inflamed mouth conditions such as gingivitis or oral mucositis.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal sage cream's antibacterial properties can help combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to inflamed mouth conditions, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, which have been shown to reduce swelling and combat infections. These properties make sage creams an effective remedy for mouth ulcers, gum inflammation, and other oral health issues. The soothing and calming effects of sage creams can also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with inflamed mouth.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus alleviating mouth inflammation.
Ursolic acid: A terpene with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm inflamed tissues in the mouth, reducing pain and discomfort.

Salvianolic acid: A water-soluble phenolic acid that exhibits strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to protect mouth tissues from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation caused by mouth infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Due to their high content of tannins and antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties make them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection in the mouth.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of Salvia officinalis contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 35-40°c for 2-3 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Mix 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis with 50 grams of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouth.

Antimicrobial properties: The menthol and other oils in peppermint creams exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to mouth inflammation.

Anesthetic and soothing effects: Peppermint's cooling and numbing sensation (thanks to the presence of camphor) helps reduce pain and discomfort, while its soothing properties calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams helps to numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants present in peppermint help to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections that can cause inflammation. The cooling sensation of peppermint creams also provides relief from irritation and itching, making it an effective remedy for mouth inflammation. This natural remedy promotes a healthy mouth and soothes irritated tissues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to numb the mouth and reduce pain associated with inflamed mouth by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the swelling and redness associated with inflamed mouth.
Limonene: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause infections and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the mouth.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain a high concentration of essential oils, including menthol and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and protect the mouth.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are a rich source of menthol and menthone, which have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth, making them a valuable component in creams for this purpose.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried mentha x piperita leaves in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Melt 100g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat to 180f within 10 minutes.
Combine the infused oil and melted beeswax in a mixing bowl and heat to 160f.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making it effective in soothing gum inflammation and mouth sores.

Kaempferol: Exhibits potent antioxidant activity, which can help combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by oral infections or injuries, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the mouth.

Hydrastis (Goldenseal's active compound): Has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate bacteria responsible for oral infections, such as gum disease and mouth sores, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis, also known as goldenseal, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The berberine content in goldenseal reduces swelling and soothes irritated tissues, while its antiseptic properties combat underlying bacterial or fungal infections that may be causing the inflammation. As a result, goldenseal creams can quickly bring relief to inflamed mouth sores, ulcers, and other conditions, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to oral care routines.
remedy_constituents: Hydrastine: It helps with inflamed mouth by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to oral infections and inflammation.
Berberine: It helps with inflamed mouth by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing swelling and promoting healing in oral tissues.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g., hydrastine's related compound hydrangenol): They help with inflamed mouth by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing pain and inflammation associated with oral conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain berberine, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps soothe mouth inflammation.
Leaves: They are also used to make creams for mouth inflammation due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of hydrastis canadensis rootlets and dry them at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried hydrastis canadensis rootlets with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the double boiler daily, at 40 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then filter it through a 5-micron filter.
Mix 100 grams of the infused oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of distilled water to create the cream base.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, providing relief from inflammation.
Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of dandelion help to combat infections that can cause inflamed mouth sores, promoting healing and preventing further infection.
Wound-healing compounds: Dandelion contains compounds like caffeic acid, which have been shown to accelerate wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis and improving tissue repair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream is derived from the roots of the plant, which contain a rich source of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce swelling and pain. The anti-inflammatory compounds in the cream help to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, providing quick relief from mouth sores, ulcers, and gum inflammation. This natural remedy can be an effective alternative to conventional treatments for mouth inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids (especially Chlorogenic acid): Chlorogenic acid helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in inflamed mouth by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in dandelion, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed mouth.
Taraxasterol (a triterpenoid saponin): Taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm inflamed mouth by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them suitable for soothing inflamed mouth tissues.
Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them useful for treating mouth ulcers and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Combine 1 cup of taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes then reduce heat and let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter with 2 tablespoons of the herbal liquid until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth by decreasing prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation.

Antibacterial: Aloe vera's antibacterial properties, including anthraquinones and salicylic acid, combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oral infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy balance of mouth flora.

Wound-healing and soothing: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help soothe and calm irritated tissues, while also facilitating the healing process by stimulating collagen production and improving skin elasticity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Applying aloe vera cream to an inflamed mouth can help to calm the discomfort and reduce redness. It can also help to protect the mucous membranes from further irritation, allowing the mouth to heal faster and feel more comfortable. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for mouth inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Gelatinous Mucilage: A complex mixture of polysaccharides that forms a protective barrier on the oral mucosa, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Aloe-emodin: A chromone compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to soothe and calm inflamed oral tissues.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin C and Manganese): These antioxidants and minerals have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce swelling and fight off infections in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of soothing compounds, such as aloe-emodin and aloin, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth.
Gel inside Leaves: It's rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which calm and soothe the inflamed tissues in the mouth.
Gel inside the inside of the Leaves: This part is particularly effective in treating mouth ulcers and gum inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 2 tablespoons of distilled water in a bowl.
Add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons of beeswax to the aloe vera mixture.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency in about 10 minutes.
Pour the herbal aloe vera cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Maidenhair Tree creams contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth.

Antimicrobial properties: The extracts of Maidenhair Tree have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, which can aid in preventing infections that contribute to inflamed mouth conditions.

Wound healing promotion: Ginkgo biloba has been found to enhance collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and promote wound healing, which may help speed up the recovery process from an inflamed mouth.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, work together to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth, soothing pain and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood circulation helps to promote healing and reduce the appearance of redness and irritation, providing quick relief for mouth inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, thereby alleviating inflamed mouth conditions.
Ginkgolides: These terpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing swelling and redness associated with inflamed mouth conditions.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, thereby providing relief from inflamed mouth conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and pain.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are known to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful in treating mouth inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves by steeping 10-15 grams in 100ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Add 10-20 grams of beeswax and 10-20 grams of shea butter to the oil mixture.
Heat the mixture to 180-190°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the beeswax and shea butter.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30-60 minutes.

remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and coat the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal marshmallow creams help combat free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, promoting healing and reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth by inhibiting enzymes that promote inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort. This natural barrier helps to calm inflamed tissues, promoting healing and reducing pain. As a result, Althaea officinalis creams are often used to treat mouth ulcers, gum inflammation, and other oral mucositis conditions, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with mouth ulcers, gum inflammation, and other oral conditions.

Polysaccharides (galactans): Polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making them effective in treating conditions such as mouth ulcers and gum inflammation.

Althaea flavonoids (mainly quercetin): Althaea flavonoids, particularly quercetin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth, making them effective in treating conditions such as mouth ulcers, gum inflammation, and other oral conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are the most commonly used part due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties for inflamed mouth tissue.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are also used to create creams for inflamed mouth due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits.
Stems: Althaea officinalis stems are utilized in some creams due to their content of mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 teaspoons of dried althaea officinalis root in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl to separate the liquid from the solids.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of the infused liquid and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil to the melted wax mixture.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency before pouring into containers to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The cream's natural oils and extracts reduce swelling and ease pain, providing relief from discomfort and making it easier to eat and speak. Additionally, fennel's antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment and speeding up the healing process. As a result, fennel creams offer effective natural remedy for inflamed mouths.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants help reduce inflammation by neutralizing free radicals and protecting the mouth tissues from damage.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help alleviate swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed mouth conditions.
Antibacterial properties: Fennel's antibacterial activity helps combat the growth of bacteria that can contribute to inflammation and infection in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, making them a popular ingredient in creams for oral inflammation.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that combat infection and alleviate inflammation in the mouth, making them a natural remedy for inflamed mouths.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them in a warm place for 3-4 weeks.
Crush 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until it reaches 60°c.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which work together to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. When applied topically, the cream forms a protective barrier on the affected area, reducing irritation and promoting healing. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antihistamine properties help to alleviate pain and itching associated with inflamed mouth tissues, providing quick relief from symptoms like redness, tenderness, and swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds like polysaccharides, flavonoids, and terpenes that reduce inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from swollen gums, cheekbones, and tongue.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle creams help protect against oxidative stress caused by inflammatory processes, which can damage tissues and contribute to chronic conditions like periodontitis and oral cancer.

Anti-allergic effects: Stinging nettle creams contain compounds that block the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions, which helps alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and swelling in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed mouth.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote healing of oral mucosa.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry urtica dioica leaves in small batches for at least two weeks to ensure optimal potency.
Grind dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Mix 20 grams of powdered urtica dioica with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated at approximately 80 degrees celsius.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool completely before storing in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herb's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, reduce swelling and pain, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation. Additionally, St John's wort has antibacterial and antiviral properties that help combat underlying infections, promoting a healthy environment in the mouth and reducing inflammation. As a result, St John's wort creams can effectively alleviate symptoms of inflamed mouth conditions, such as gingivitis, mouth ulcers, and oral mucositis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can soothe inflamed tissues and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with oral inflammation.

Antimicrobial properties: St. John's Wort creams may exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause oral infections and inflammation, thereby helping to prevent secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for reducing swelling and pain in the mouth.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth.
Leaves: With their high content of triterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, they are useful for reducing inflammation and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and dry them using shade or air drying method for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and sift through a mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Combine 10g of the powdered herb with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency that is slightly firm to the touch and pour into sterilized glass jars.
Label and store the creams in a dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months, shaking well before use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly creams helps with inflamed mouth because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The creams' herbal ingredients, such as chamomile and calendula, work to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by inflamed mouths. Additionally, the creams' antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment and speeding up the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal dog holly creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth.
Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to the inflammation and discomfort in the mouth.
Wound-healing agents: Herbal dog holly creams contain saponins, which have been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, dog holly roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against oral infections and reducing swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome's bitter and astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the mouth, while its antimicrobial activity combats infection-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ruscus aculeatus roots extract in ethanol solvent ratio of 1:2 for at least 24 hours.
Filter the extracted solution through cheesecloth to separate liquid from solids and discard the residue immediately.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler with gentle heat until melted completely.
Combine 10 grams of ruscus aculeatus extract, 5 grams of aloe vera gel, and 2 drops of tea tree essential oil into the melted wax mixture slowly.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The herbal extract contains apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-induced inflammation. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties combat harmful bacteria and viruses that can exacerbate mouth sores and infections, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in an inflamed mouth by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and damage in the mouth, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of complications like gum disease.

Calming effects on mucous membranes: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties help relax inflamed mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing discomfort, pain, and redness associated with conditions like aphthous stomatitis or mouth ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the mouth.
Flowers: Contain azulene, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that calms and cools inflamed areas in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly in the shade for at least two weeks.
Grind dried chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered chamomilla with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and let it macerate for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the solid particles and discard them.
Mix the filtered liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of cocoa butter, then heat the mixture gently until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb has been used for centuries to calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling in the mouth. Its cooling effect provides instant relief from pain and discomfort caused by inflammation, making it an effective treatment for conditions such as canker sores and gingivitis. Additionally, cornflower's antibacterial properties help to prevent infection, promoting a healthy oral environment and speeding up the healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cornflower contains flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth.
Antimicrobial properties: The herb has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria and fungi that can cause oral infections, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Wound-healing agents: Cornflower contains saponins and phenolic acids that have been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe mucous membranes, making them effective for treating inflamed mouth.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate pain and discomfort caused by an inflamed mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh centaurea cyanus flowers on a sunny morning in july.
Dry the flowers for 2 weeks under shade or using a food dehydrator at 60°c.
Crush 20 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of crushed centaurea cyanus with 50 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain caused by inflammation. The plantain extract in these creams has been shown to have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, plantain's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and viruses that can contribute to oral infections, promoting healing and preventing further irritation. As a result, plantain creams offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating inflamed mouth symptoms, providing quick and lasting relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain cream contains compounds like allantoin, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial properties of plantain cream, such as the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids, help to combat bacterial infections that can cause mouth sores and inflammation.

Wound-healing properties: Plantain's mucilages (gel-like substances) create a protective barrier on the skin or mucous membranes, promoting wound healing by keeping the affected area moist and shielding it from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated oral tissues.
Barks: Contains salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling associated with inflamed mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sufficient amount of plantago major leaves.
Grind dried plantago major leaves into fine powder.
Mix ground plantago major with carrier oils and beeswax.
Heat mixture until beeswax melts and ingredients combine smoothly.
Cool and whip the cream to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with inflamed mouth because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredient, isoflavones, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflammation in the mouth. Additionally, red clover's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and viruses that can exacerbate oral inflammation. As a natural remedy, it soothes and calms irritated tissue, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Red clover cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in an inflamed mouth, allowing for a more comfortable and normal eating and speaking experience.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in red clover cream help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and tissue damage in the mouth, promoting healing and regeneration of healthy tissues.

Wound-healing: Red clover's saponins and flavonoids have been shown to enhance wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood vessel formation, and reducing oxidative stress, all of which contribute to faster and more effective recovery from oral mucosal lesions and ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots are rich in flavonoids and triterpenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe oral mucositis.
Barks: The barks of red clover contain phenolic acids and tannins that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacterial infections causing mouth inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh red clover flowers trifolium pratense in the morning after dew has dried off.
Dry the harvested flowers using a warm air circulation oven at 30°c for 24 hours.
Create an infusion by mixing 20g of dried red clover flowers with 200ml boiling distilled water and steeping for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container then add 10g of beeswax and 2% tea tree essential oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat stirring occasionally until the beeswax has fully dissolved and the cream has cooled to around 60°c.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for inflamed mouth are topical remedies infused with natural herbs and essential oils that help soothe and calm irritated oral tissues. They typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil. Examples of herbal creams for inflamed mouth include aloe vera gel and chamomile cream, which can provide relief from conditions like mouth ulcers, gum inflammation, and sore throats, improving daily lives by reducing discomfort and promoting oral health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed mouth that are treated with herbal creams are various oral and skin conditions. These include gum infections, periodontitis, aphthous ulcers, cold sores, acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties that help to reduce pain, swelling, and redness associated with these conditions, promoting healing and relief from discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with inflamed mouth is a blend of Aloe vera, Chamomile, and Echinacea. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the mouth, while Chamomile's antiseptic qualities reduce swelling and fight infection. Echinacea, rich in antioxidants, boosts the immune system and accelerates healing. Applying a combination of these herbal creams can provide effective relief from mouth inflammation, promoting a healthy and pain-free oral environment. Regular use can also help prevent future occurrences of inflamed mouth.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of Education and Health Promotion, herbal creams can be good for inflamed mouths due to their effectiveness and safety in treating oral diseases. The study, which conducted a systematic review of 49 original research studies on the use of medicinal plants and herbal products for oral health care, found that herbal product interventions were found to be effective and safe alternatives for oral health care. Specifically, herbal creams, such as those made from chamomile and Aloe vera, which were the most frequently reported herbal compounds, can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth.
